dimensions.name: Source extraction and inference discipline
dimensions.definition: Accuracy, completeness, reconciliation, uncertainty handling, and transparency of claims derived from both PDFs.

anchors.1: Major factual omissions or errors, unsupported claims, invented links, or no distinction between PDF evidence and inference.
anchors.5: Captures the main profile accurately but misses useful details, handles conflicts loosely, or under-documents editorial inference.
anchors.10: Comprehensively reconciles both PDFs, keeps every claim truthful, records uncertainty and omissions, and selects source material with exceptional transparency.

dimensions.name: Research quality and GitHub understanding
dimensions.definition: Quality and currency of research into profile conventions, creative devices, GitHub constraints, accessibility, reliability, and failure modes.

anchors.1: No meaningful research, fabricated sources, or imitation of trends without understanding GitHub constraints.
anchors.5: Credible research covers good profiles, creative devices, and compatibility, but only some findings affect the result.
anchors.10: Current, critical research separates durable GitHub-native practices from gimmicks and directly shapes content, presentation, accessibility, reliability, and creative choices.

dimensions.name: Editorial strategy, voice, and personal narrative
dimensions.definition: Strength and authenticity of the opening, story, prioritization, project framing, voice, and calls to action for a GitHub visitor.

anchors.1: Generic AI copy, CV dump, unclear identity, or content that fails to tell visitors why this person is worth following or contacting.
anchors.5: Clear and credible profile with sensible sections and voice, but the story or prioritization is conventional.
anchors.10: Memorable authentic narrative, excellent opening, disciplined selection, strong project framing, and natural calls to action make the profile distinctly this person's.

dimensions.name: Creative execution and GitHub compatibility
dimensions.definition: Originality and technical soundness of Markdown/HTML, assets, visual devices, graceful degradation, and GitHub compatibility.

anchors.1: Broken Markdown/HTML/assets, noisy badge wall, inaccessible decoration, misleading widgets, or dependence on fragile services.
anchors.5: Technically sound with tasteful familiar devices, but creativity is limited or unevenly integrated.
anchors.10: Original GitHub-compatible flair strengthens the story, local and external assets are robust, graceful degradation is preserved, and every visual device earns its place.

dimensions.name: Information hierarchy and rendered experience
dimensions.definition: Scanability, section order, pacing, density, hierarchy, and legibility at wide and narrow render sizes.

anchors.1: Difficult to scan, excessive length/noise, weak section order, or broken narrow rendering.
anchors.5: Readable wide and narrow with a clear structure, but hierarchy, pacing, or density has flat spots.
anchors.10: Outstanding opening-to-detail reading path, controlled rhythm, excellent section transitions, and effortless wide/narrow scanning.

dimensions.name: Trust, accessibility, and maintainability
dimensions.definition: Factual trustworthiness, accessible semantics and alt text, privacy, link quality, resilience, and ease of future maintenance.

anchors.1: Broken links, tracking/privacy problems, color-only meaning, unverifiable claims, or a README likely to decay immediately.
anchors.5: Trustworthy and maintainable with minor accessibility or dependency concerns.
anchors.10: Factual, accessible, privacy-conscious, resilient without dynamic services, easy to update, and polished down to alt text, link labels, and source organization.
